_comment: Trigger-eval cases for skills/segment-and-analyze/SKILL.md. Each case has a prompt and a reason explaining the expected routing decision.

should_fire.prompt: Measure crack width in these bridge inspection photos for our maintenance report
should_fire.reason: Crack width measurement requires pixel-precise mask + skeleton cross-section — core segment-and-analyze capability

should_fire.prompt: Segment tumor regions in these MRI scans and measure their area in mm²
should_fire.reason: Tumor area measurement in physical units — instance segmentation + calibrated area output

should_fire.prompt: I need pixel-precise outlines of corrosion patches on ship hull images
should_fire.reason: Pixel-precise outline = instance mask — explicit segmentation trigger phrase

should_fire.prompt: Measure the exact area of each defect found in solar panel images
should_fire.reason: Exact area measurement per defect instance — requires mask, not bbox; segment-and-analyze

should_fire.prompt: Detect and outline each part on the assembly line, then measure their dimensions
should_fire.reason: Outline + measure dimensions — contour and area measurement from masks; not a simple count

should_fire.prompt: I want to quantify corrosion area on steel beams from inspection photos
should_fire.reason: Corrosion area quantification — defect area measurement from mask; segment-and-analyze scope

should_fire.prompt: Get the contour of every lesion in these dermoscopy images and compute their shape
should_fire.reason: Contour extraction + shape analysis (lesion boundary) — instance segmentation trigger

should_fire.prompt: Count pixels belonging to each crack and give me area measurements in mm squared
should_fire.reason: Count pixels = pixel mask area measurement — explicit pixel counting + mm² output

should_not_fire.prompt: Count how many defects appear on each board
should_not_fire.reason: Per-instance count without area measurement — routes to detect-and-analyze, not segment-and-analyze

should_not_fire.prompt: Is this solar panel defective? Flag the fail images
should_not_fire.reason: Image-level verdict (defective yes/no for whole image) — routes to classify-or-flag; no masks needed

should_not_fire.prompt: Read the serial number stamped on this industrial part
should_not_fire.reason: Text extraction / OCR task — routes to read-text; no segmentation involved

should_not_fire.prompt: Track workers as they move through the factory floor
should_not_fire.reason: Identity-linked tracking across frames — routes to track-and-count, not segment-and-analyze

should_not_fire.prompt: What does managed deployment cost for 3 camera streams running 24/7?
should_not_fire.reason: Cost/scale question with no unsolved segmentation problem — routes to deployment-consultant

should_not_fire.prompt: I already have masks from my annotation tool — how do I post-process them to count objects?
should_not_fire.reason: User already has masks and asks only about post-processing — explicitly out of scope for this skill

should_not_fire.prompt: Classify each whole image as pass or fail for our QA line
should_not_fire.reason: Image-level classification (pass/fail verdict on the whole image) — routes to classify-or-flag; no per-instance masks

should_not_fire.prompt: Extract and OCR all the text labels visible on these product packages
should_not_fire.reason: OCR / text extraction task — routes to read-text; not a segmentation problem
